title: Five Wishes Before Thunder
synopsis: Ember the baby dragon discovers mysterious helpers on a pebble beach as a summer storm approaches, learning that the best adventures happen when you put down your tablet and notice the world around you.

pages.text: Pop-pop-pop go the raindrops on my tablet screen! I'm sitting on the pebble beach, trying to watch my favorite show, but the drops keep making funny patterns on the glass. Splish-splash-splish! The waves are getting bigger and bigger, rolling up the smooth stones with a rumble-tumble sound. My tablet makes a beep-beep-beep noise - only five minutes of battery left! The storm clouds are getting darker and darker, swirling like whipped cream in the sky. But wait... there's something sparkly tucked under the driftwood log next to me. A beautiful blue shell that wasn't there before! Who could have left it? I look around the empty beach, but I don't see anyone. The shell gleams like a jewel against the gray pebbles.

illustration.description: Chalkboard style drawing of a small friendly baby dragon with coral-red scales, a cream belly, tiny folded wings, a curly tail, and big warm amber eyes sitting on a pebble beach holding a tablet. A blue shell sparkles near driftwood. Storm clouds gather overhead. White and colored chalk on dark green board with dusty texture, equations doodles, school themed, artistic warm, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Ember sitting on the beach with a tablet as a storm approaches, discovering a mysterious blue shell

pages.text: Beep-beep-beep! Four minutes left on my tablet now. The wind is whoosh-whooshing stronger, making my tiny wings flutter-flutter like little flags. I put the beautiful blue shell in my pocket and look at my tablet again, but the screen is getting harder to see with all these raindrops! Pitter-patter-pitter! Then I notice something amazing - there's a perfect circle of the smoothest, roundest pebbles right where I'm sitting. They're all different colors: purple and pink and gold and silver! These weren't here when I first sat down, I'm sure of it. Someone must have arranged them just for me! But who? Who? Who? I look left and right, up and down the beach. The waves crash-splash against the rocks, but I don't see a single person anywhere.

illustration.description: Chalkboard style drawing of a small friendly baby dragon with coral-red scales, a cream belly, tiny folded wings, a curly tail, and big warm amber eyes surrounded by a perfect circle of colorful pebbles on the beach. Wind blows through the scene with storm clouds darker overhead. White and colored chalk on dark green board with dusty texture, equations doodles, school themed, artistic warm, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Ember discovering a mysterious circle of colorful pebbles around him on the windy beach

pages.text: Three minutes left! Beep-beep-beep goes my tablet, but I'm starting to forget about my show. The thunder goes rumble-rumble-RUMBLE in the distance, and the pebbles under my feet go click-clack-click as the waves pull them back and forth. I stand up to stretch my curly tail, and - oh my! - there's a tiny sailboat made of driftwood and a leaf, sitting perfectly in a tide pool right behind me! It bobs up and down, up and down in the shallow water. The little boat has my initial 'E' carved into its side! Someone made this just for me, but who could it be? I look all around the beach again - behind the big rocks, near the seaweed, by the lighthouse in the distance. Nobody, nobody, nobody! But someone is definitely here, helping me, leaving me treasures! My heart goes thump-thump-thump with excitement.

illustration.description: Chalkboard style drawing of a small friendly baby dragon with coral-red scales, a cream belly, tiny folded wings, a curly tail, and big warm amber eyes looking at a tiny sailboat in a tide pool. The boat has the letter 'E' carved on it. Storm is getting closer with darker clouds. White and colored chalk on dark green board with dusty texture, equations doodles, school themed, artistic warm, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Ember discovering a tiny sailboat with his initial carved into it floating in a tide pool

pages.text: Only two minutes left! Beep-beep! But I barely hear my tablet anymore because listen-listen-listen - the storm is making the most beautiful music! The wind whistles whoo-whoo-whoo through the rocks, the waves go crash-whoosh-tinkle on the pebbles, and the rain goes plink-plink-plink like tiny bells! I close my tablet and put it safely in my backpack. That's when I see the most wonderful thing yet - a rainbow of sea glass pieces spelling out 'WELCOME HOME' right there in the wet sand! Red and green and blue and yellow pieces, all smooth and glowing! They must have been placed there while I was looking at the sailboat. My secret helper is still here somewhere! I spin around and around, my coral-red scales shimmering in the stormy light. 'Thank you, whoever you are!' I call out into the wind.

illustration.description: Chalkboard style drawing of a small friendly baby dragon with coral-red scales, a cream belly, tiny folded wings, a curly tail, and big warm amber eyes looking at colorful sea glass pieces spelling 'WELCOME HOME' in the sand. The tablet is closed and put away. Storm clouds are very dark now. White and colored chalk on dark green board with dusty texture, equations doodles, school themed, artistic warm, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Ember discovering sea glass spelling 'WELCOME HOME' as he puts his tablet away during the storm

pages.text: One minute left on my tablet, but I don't even think about turning it back on! The storm is here now - whoosh-CRASH-pitter-patter-RUMBLE! Lightning goes flash-flash-flash across the sky, and I see something magical in that bright light. Footprints! Footprints in the sand that look just like... mine! Tiny dragon prints with little claw marks, curly tail drags, and wing-tip touches. They make a circle all around where I've been sitting, and they lead to each treasure I found! The blue shell, the colorful pebbles, the sailboat, the sea glass message. My secret helper... is me! I've been so busy looking at my screen that I forgot I was walking around, gathering treasures, making this beautiful circle of magic! The storm swirls around me, and I spread my tiny wings wide, feeling the rain and wind and thunder in my whole dragon heart. I am my own best friend!

illustration.description: Chalkboard style drawing of a small friendly baby dragon with coral-red scales, a cream belly, tiny folded wings, a curly tail, and big warm amber eyes with wings spread wide in the storm, looking at his own footprints in the sand. Lightning illuminates the scene with all the treasures visible around him. White and colored chalk on dark green board with dusty texture, equations doodles, school themed, artistic warm, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Ember with wings spread wide in the storm, discovering his own footprints and realizing he was his own secret helper

metadata.educationalValue: Encourages children to engage with their environment and discover that they can be their own source of wonder and adventure
metadata.parentingTip: This story opens conversations about balancing screen time with outdoor exploration and self-directed play.

coverPrompt.description: Chalkboard style drawing showing a small friendly baby dragon with coral-red scales, a cream belly, tiny folded wings, a curly tail, and big warm amber eyes standing on a pebble beach with storm clouds gathering overhead. White and colored chalk on dark green board with dusty texture and artistic warm feeling.
coverPrompt.alt: A baby dragon on a beach with storm clouds approaching

questions.question: What happens to Ember's tablet during the storm?
choices: It gets wet from the rain
questions.explanation: Ember's tablet keeps beeping because the battery is running down, counting from five minutes to one minute.

questions.question: What was the first treasure Ember found?
questions.explanation: The first treasure Ember noticed was a sparkly blue shell tucked under the driftwood log.

questions.question: What did the sea glass pieces spell out?
questions.explanation: The rainbow of sea glass pieces spelled out 'WELCOME HOME' in the wet sand.

questions.question: Who was Ember's secret helper?
questions.explanation: Ember discovered that he was his own secret helper - he had been creating the treasures while distracted by his tablet.

questions.question: How does Ember feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Ember spreads his wings wide and feels the storm in his whole dragon heart, realizing he is his own best friend.
